title: Goan Sannas
description: fluffy, mildly sweet steamed rice cakes that are a staple in Goan and Mangalorean cuisine.

ingredients.title: For the batter
items: 1 cup Goan parboiled rice (ukda rice) – washed and soaked overnight
¼ cup grated coconut (fresh, or frozen thawed)
OR substitute with: ½ tsp instant yeast + ¼ tsp sugar dissolved in 2 tbsp warm water

ingredients.title: For finishing (optional, fine dining twist)
items: A drizzle of coconut milk reduction
Garnish: toasted sesame seeds or micro herbs/edible flowers

instruction.title: 1. Prepare Fermentation Starter
steps: If using yeast: mix yeast + sugar in warm water, allow to froth (10 mins).

instruction.title: 2. Grind Batter
steps: Grind with grated coconut and cooked rice into a smooth, thick batter (medium-pouring consistency).

instruction.title: 3. Ferment
steps: Stir in salt, sugar, and toddy/yeast mixture.
Cover and ferment 6–8 hours (overnight) in a warm spot until doubled and bubbly.

instruction.title: 4. Steam Sannas
steps: Grease moulds (idli moulds, steel bowls, or ramekins).
Pour batter ¾ full (to allow rising).
Steam gently for 12–15 mins. Check with a toothpick.

instruction.title: 5. Presentation
steps: Serve warm, brushed with a little coconut milk reduction for shine.
Garnish with sesame seeds or edible flowers.
Pair with Goan sorpotel, pork vindaloo, or prawn curry for authenticity.

chefTips: Rice Choice: Use Goan parboiled ukda rice for authentic texture; regular parboiled rice works but is less aromatic.
Fermentation: Toddy gives traditional flavor—if unavailable, use yeast but add a touch of coconut water for depth.
Texture Goal: Light, fluffy, slightly spongy with a mild sweetness.
Fine Dining Twist: Steam in uniform moulds for consistent plating. Glaze with coconut milk reduction to add shine and subtle richness.
